hero.lead: Receive, process, and export telemetry data. A vendor-agnostic agent for ingesting, processing, and exporting metrics, traces, and logs.

pipeline.title: Pipeline anatomy
pipeline.lead: A Collector pipeline is composed of receivers, processors, exporters, connectors, and extensions. Click any stage to filter the components list.

most-used.title: Most-used components
most-used.description: Receivers, processors, and exporters most commonly deployed.

core-vs-contrib.title: Core vs. Contrib
core-vs-contrib.description: Compare the core distribution against the contrib add-ons.

diff.title: Diff across versions
diff.description: See what changed between two Collector releases.

page.title: OpenTelemetry Collector
page.description: A vendor-agnostic implementation for receiving, processing, and exporting telemetry data.

header.title: Collector
header.description: Navigate the OpenTelemetry Collector ecosystem. Discover receivers, processors, exporters, and extensions across different distributions.
header.comingSoon: We're currently building the Collector component explorer. Stay tuned for a comprehensive view of receivers, processors, and more!

card.defaultDescription: Browse technical details and configuration options for this component.

empty.title: No components found
empty.description: We couldn't find any components matching your search criteria.

typeDescriptions.receiver: Receivers collect telemetry data from various sources and formats.
typeDescriptions.processor: Processors transform, filter, or enrich telemetry data between receivers and exporters.
typeDescriptions.exporter: Exporters send telemetry data to one or more backends or destinations.
typeDescriptions.extension: Extensions provide additional capabilities to the collector without processing telemetry data.
typeDescriptions.connector: Connectors act as both an exporter and a receiver, joining two pipelines together.

stabilityDescriptions.development: Not all pieces of the component are in place yet. The component should not be used in production.
stabilityDescriptions.alpha: The component is ready to be used for limited non-critical workloads and the authors of this component would welcome your feedback.
stabilityDescriptions.beta: Same as Alpha, but the configuration options are deemed stable. Suitable for broader usage.
stabilityDescriptions.stable: The component is ready for general availability. Breaking changes are not expected to happen without prior notice.
stabilityDescriptions.deprecated: The component is planned to be removed in a future version and no further support will be provided.
stabilityDescriptions.unmaintained: The component is no longer actively maintained. New issues will likely not be worked on.

distributions.packaged: This component is packaged within the following distributions:
distributions.noInfo: No distribution information is currently available for this component.

contrib.name: OpenTelemetry Collector Contrib
contrib.desc: The community-driven distribution containing third-party plugins, specialized receivers, and experimental components.

core.name: OpenTelemetry Collector Core
core.desc: The core distribution containing only the most essential, officially supported telemetry components.

k8s.name: OpenTelemetry Operator for Kubernetes
k8s.desc: The official Kubernetes Operator designed to manage and provision the OpenTelemetry Collector.

detail.noStability: No stability information available for this version.

receiver.label: Receiver
receiver.description: Collect telemetry data from various sources including OTLP, Prometheus, Jaeger, and many others

processor.label: Processor
processor.description: Transform, filter, batch, and enrich telemetry data before export

exporter.label: Exporter
exporter.description: Send telemetry data to observability backends and storage systems

extension.label: Extension
extension.description: Provide additional capabilities like health checks, profiling, and authentication

connector.label: Connector
connector.description: Connect multiple pipelines and enable data flow between signal types

core.description: Officially maintained components with stable APIs. Minimal dependencies, production-ready.

contrib.description: Community-contributed components. May have varying stability levels. Broader ecosystem coverage.
